goal: Grow a large game catalog and cover layer without turning price tracking into a mass-refresh problem.

catalogTiers.purpose: Validate taste, memory, explanations, and first-session value.

catalogTiers.refreshCadence: weekly metadata refresh, on-demand cover repair
catalogTiers.purpose: Cover popular, current, subscription, wishlist, and adjacent recommendation candidates.

catalogTiers.refreshCadence: lazy import on user search or source change
catalogTiers.purpose: Let users find obscure games without paying to refresh the whole market.

providers.commercialUse: RAWG API terms list free commercial use for startups and hobby projects within request/product limits, with attribution and active source links. Re-check account terms before public launch.
providers.coverUse: Primary early cover candidate source. Store remote image URLs, sourceUrl, and attribution; do not cache originals in the prototype.
providers.notes: Good for broad discovery, descriptions, tags, screenshots, and initial cover candidates. If terms change or scale exceeds limits, keep generated posters and switch to a paid/partner source.

providers.commercialUse: Free non-commercial API; commercial need should go through partnership.
providers.coverUse: Useful for rich metadata and covers after terms are cleared.
providers.notes: Strong normalized game database, but commercial path must be clarified before production.

providers.coverUse: Good cover and screenshot depth, especially for historical catalog quality.
providers.notes: Better as a paid enrichment source after the product signal is proven.

providers.commercialUse: Artwork-specific source; terms need review before product use.
providers.coverUse: Use as a repair layer for missing or poor covers, not as canonical metadata.
providers.notes: Useful for grids, heroes, logos, and user-friendly library visuals.

providers.coverUse: Prefer store-provided art when the user is looking at that platform context.
providers.notes: Best for platform identity, editions, and release pages; do not confuse with price freshness.

selectedPath.when: Always available for every catalog record.
selectedPath.purpose: Keep the catalog visually usable before any external image is resolved.

selectedPath.when: RAWG_API_KEY is present and the title match has a background image.
selectedPath.purpose: Attach a real remote cover candidate with RAWG attribution and sourceUrl.

selectedPath.when: A human accepts the candidate match and source context.
selectedPath.purpose: Prevent wrong-title cover matches from becoming trusted product UI.

selectedPath.when: RAWG misses and IGDB commercial/partnership path is cleared.
selectedPath.purpose: Use a stronger normalized database as a later repair layer.

attribution.rawg: Show RAWG as the source and link to sourceUrl wherever a RAWG image is displayed.
attribution.generated_poster: Generated locally; no third-party artwork is used.
attribution.official_store: Show the platform/store context and never imply cross-platform official status.

rules: Never claim an image is official unless the source marks it as official or it comes from an official store record.
Keep attribution and source URL next to every imported cover record.
Use generated placeholder art when cover rights or source confidence are unclear.
Treat cover freshness separately from price and subscription freshness.

trustRules: Catalog metadata can be stale longer than prices; price and subscription claims need stricter freshness.
AI atomization may enrich catalog records, but it cannot invent release dates, platforms, prices, or availability.
Large catalog growth should be lazy and intent-led: wishlist, search, subscription, upcoming, and similar-game expansion first.
A missing cover is acceptable; a misleading cover or wrong game match is a trust failure.
